Video.summary: “The Century Race”: Dale believes he found the bike that will help him beat a rival in an annual race of 100 year old motorcycles. But will it run?
Video.title: The Century Race

Video.summary: A California collector gambles on a big payday from his Crocker/Caddy combo.
Video.title: Doubling Down

Video.summary: “A Motorcycle Crime Story”: The horrific tale of a double murder over money and motorcycles.
Video.title: Motorcycle Crime Story

Video.summary: An family farm in the mid-west contains houses, barns, outbuildings, trailers, grain silos and buses full of old stuff and perhaps the biggest stash of undiscovered old motorcycles in the country. The collection was put together by their unnamed father. Seller agrees to have Dale broker rare Excelsior for high-end client. Dale has the buyer, but will he outbid his own client for the exclusive find?
Video.title: Diamond in Dodge City

Video.summary: Matt is out for revenge. Last year Dale beat him badly at the Annual Vintage bike race in Barber, Alabama. But this year there is a whole new tale to tell. With just days until the race - Dale is secretly struggling to get the bike he beat Matt with last year up and working. Matt struggles just to locate a 100-year old bike. Matt's prayers are answered thanks to an old Catholic Priest he located who has possession of a bike. The priest might let Matt have the bike to race - but there are conditions. The toughest one: Don't tell Dale you have it. Can Matt afford to buy the bike and get it running in time for the race without Dale seeing it? Will Dale even get his broken bike started? And if they ever get to race, will Matt exact his revenge?
Video.title: Don't Tell Dale

Video.summary: “12hrs. & Running”: Dale turns a surprise 60th birthday party into a challenge to his guests to start 120 of the rarest motorcycles in the world.
Video.title: 12 Hours and Running

Video.summary: A cross-country race to lay claim to some of the world’s most sought after works specials.
Video.title: Hidden Hillclimbers

Video.summary: A vague tip leads Matt and Dale into a WWII era military warehouse and beyond.
Video.title: Peashooter

Video.summary: A thousand bulging barns of choppers, bobbers, and board trackers are emptied and on display in the Quad Cities.
Video.title: Davenport Swap

Video.summary: “Yes, Dale there is a 100 Year Old Harley”: Dale hopes his labor of love becomes a prized possession when a six-figure Harley goes to auction.
Video.title: 100 Year Old Harley

Video.summary: Daimlers and Morgans, and Citroens, oh my! Caffeine & Octane is known for having something for everyone. That reputation grows stronger as Bob Boston’s collection, on the lunatic fringe of automotive, rattles into an event; guest host Katherine Legge explains the competitor to her racing sponsor Acura, Porsche.
Video.title: Boston's (Un)Common Collection

Video.summary: Early spring in Jekyll Island, GA is something to behold. Add a few hundred of the country’s coolest rides for Caffeine & Octane at the Beach, and it’s a thing of beauty. Models that blasted the glass ceiling of expectation at the all makes and models show include a hand-built, twin-turbo Tucker, a one-off 1951 Kaiser Drag’N, and a Roadkill Custom known only as Blasphemi!
Video.title: C&O at the Beach

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane is back at the beach on Jekyll Island with a focus on cars and families. Fathers & sons, with their barn-find Pilot House trucks and Plymouth Satellites, call cars the glue that keeps them bonded together with C&O being the best place to show them off.
Video.title: Kenny Rogers' Family Values

Video.summary: On this edition of Caffeine & Octane, we’ll go inside the head of Master Builder Bryan Fuller at the SEMA car show, the largest annual trade show in the country; SEMA always brings out the best in the automotive industry. From a jaw dropping Javelin to a multimillion-dollar Pagani, Brian will prove to you, what happens in Vegas, doesn’t always stay in Vegas! Ryan Hardwick guest hosts and ventures deep into the C&O lots to find his favorite ride of the day.
Video.title: SEMA: Inside Fuller's Head

Video.summary: Take apart and assemble a motor in less than 30 minutes? Sure, NASCAR pit crews can make the claim, but can a rag tag group of kids with names like Jelly Donut, Butter Bean, and Wonder Woman claim the pit fame? Host Rickey Gadson heads to the Sims Academy to settle a bet. This ultra fast half-hour edition of Caffeine & Octane also includes a builder’s take on an Ultra Four Unlimited Off-Road Racer; Ryan Hardwick joins the gang at Caffeine and Octane.
Video.title: Motor Masters

Video.summary: Demetrio Montalvo likes American music, American food, and especially American cars. Only one problem - Demetrio lives in Cuba. A Visa, bestowed to him from some American friends in high places, lets him travel to the States to search for tough to find automotive parts and attend his favorite car show. Showing up to Caffeine & Octane in a cool caddy is the easy part. Picking parts to take back home presents a unique set of challenges for the Cuban car builder; Formula Drift racer Federico Sceriffo brings the first ever Formula Drift Ferrari to C&O.
Video.title: Viva Cuba!

Video.summary: Porsche collector, customizer, and Urban Outlaw, Magnus Walker is a world-wide rock star to fans of the German car company. So, when Caffeine & Octane producers invited him to travel cross country to tell the story of a hidden Porsche collection tangled in a North Georgia Vineyard, they were surprised when he signed on. No sour grapes here, what Walker found at the Wolf Mountain Winery will impress even those with the most refined palette for Porsches.
Video.title: Magnus Walker's Secret Porsche Collection

Video.summary: A Hall of Famer?s search for an Exotic Car that fits; a blind man?s ?touchy feely? approach to seeing great cars.
Video.title: Chipper Jones' International Car Show

Video.summary: Back to the future with a Majestic build from Fuller Moto that showcases 3-D printed parts, an electric motor, and hub-centric steering.
Video.title: Fuller's "2029" Majestic Build

Video.summary: Horsepower and million-dollar sticker shock, from two and four wheels, dominate this edition of C & O.
Video.title: The Fastest Man on Two Wheels

Image.alt: The Fastest Man on Two Wheels
The Fastest Man on Two Wheels
The Fastest Man on Two Wheels

Video.summary: A need for speed in a Sleepy Georgia town has engineers pushing the boundaries to exceed 3000 horsepower.
Video.title: Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP

Image.alt: Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP
Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP
Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP

Video.summary: Turn of the century cars?with wooden wheels?at 85mph?oh my!
Video.title: Turn of the Century Cars

Video.summary: Urban Outlaw and designer Magnus Walker weighs in on his beloved Porsche and the legend of Hot Wheels.
Video.title: Hot Wheel's & an Urban Outlaw

Video.summary: Tracing the origins and solving the mystery of a controversial all-aluminum bodied 512 BBI Boxer Ferrari.
Video.title: Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari

Image.alt: Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari
Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari
Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane goes Turbo in the Twin Cities; Ferrari Drifter Federico Sceriffo finds a home in Atlanta.
Video.title: Twin City Turbos

Video.summary: Jaguar F-Type Project 7 crosses the Pond.
Video.title: 470MPH with Custom King George Poteet

Video.summary: Nick Rutter provides valuable tips and techniques for properly washing your vehicle to avoid instilling swirl marks
Video.title: Exterior Car Care

Video.summary: Nick Rutter teaches how to restore headlights, clean engine, remove water spots and other tips on a used car
Video.title: Restoring A Used Car

Video.summary: Bruno and Matt bring a 1972 Murdered Out Chevy pickup back from the dead by adding a new front clip and upgrading the brakes.
Video.title: Back From the Dead

Video.summary: Today we find the limits to a stock four-wheel drive jeep. As we head out to the Eaton Proving Ground in Marshall, Michigan.
Video.title: Eaton Proving Ground

Video.summary: We’ll show what to do when you’re stranded on the side of the road with an overheating problem and teach the right way diagnose and fix the problem.
Video.title: Overheating

Video.summary: Beginner to Expert, we’ve got it all. We take a 2011 Ford and show how some jobs can be easier than others
Video.title: Truck Add Ons

Video.summary: Matt takes a trip to Joplin, Missouri to check in with the guys at Chrome Shop Mafia and find out what makes the Freightliner Victory Lane Edition so unique.
Video.title: Victory Lane Edition

Video.summary: LB7 injectors fail on GM engines all the time. Today we’ll show you why you should do the repair yourself and save thousands in the process.
Video.title: Duramax Injector Repair

Video.summary: Bruno takes a trip to Surf City Garage and learns some techniques from the pros that can help you save money on your next project.
Video.title: Save the Paint

Video.summary: Matt and Bruno work on a truck that belongs to a tournament fisherman who uses his truck for work and play. It racks up a lot of miles. They are going to hook this boat captain up with some new products that will make his life a lot easier on the road!
Video.title: Paid to Play

Video.summary: Blues legend Joe Bonamassa is looking for a surprise gift for his parents. With his love of cars and guitars, Joe has to get Ray and Mr. H involved. From Mopar muscle to Mr. H’s man cave to a concert performance from Joe, this episode has a lot of Drive.
Video.title: SMOKIN JOE

Video.summary: In the early 1900s, race tracks built of wood sprang up across the country drawing crowds of 50,000 and more. Ray and Xtreme sports superstar Travis Pastrana break down the Battle on the Boards.
Video.title: BOARD TRACK BATTLES

Video.summary: Ray finds an old T-Bucket that made a 133 mph pass at the Bonneville Salt Flats in 1951. The team brings the Woody Lee T back to life and attempts to match its original speed.
Video.title: MODEL T

Video.summary: Ray finds a Gurney Eagle in a garage on Long Island. He wants to put the coveted machine back on the track at Indy to honor Dan Gurney. After intense restoration and scouring the globe for a Gurney-Westlake engine, the Eagle soars again.
Video.title: DAN GURNEY

Video.summary: Ray uncovers the infamous “Brawner Hawk” – from the Dean Van Lines team – that became the breakout car in the career of Mario Andretti. Ray enlists HOF mechanic Jim McGee to restore the vehicle and, along the way, discovers a replica with many of the car’s original parts. After an award winning stop at Concours Amelia Island, the car heads to the 100th running of Indy 500 where Mario Andretti himself gets behind the wheel.
Video.title: VINTAGE RACING

Video.summary: Dale Earnhardt Jr. wants Ray to help restore an El Camino, except add on a Laguna front end. It’s not a typical restoration, but it’s certainly a NASCAR performance-worthy restoration. Ray and the team build the car and then, in a bit of gamesmanship, challenge Kyle Tucker, owner of Detroit Speed, to a series of races against his award winning Camaro. But as always, Ray has an ace up his sleeve in Al Unser, Jr. as his driver.
Video.title: TWO JUNIORS

Video.summary: In the late 60s, Camaros, Mustangs, and Corvettes roared to the forefront of the muscle car world. When Ray gets offered a spot in a vintage race featuring these cars, he’s propelled to dive into their history and afforded a chance to get behind the wheel at the Brickyard. But because it is a pro-am race, Ray pulls in a partner – none other than Bill Elliott, aka “Awesome Bill from Dawsonville.”
Video.title: MACHINE MAGIC MARIO

Video.summary: Everyone has a car they fell in love with. Maybe it was the first? Maybe it was the hot rod the guy down the street owned? In Ray’s case, it was the ’58 Chevy he saw in the iconic coming of age car movie, American Graffiti. When Ray gets the chance to own the car after years and years of pursuit, he jumps on it. But restoring the car to its "movie state" proves to be a grueling task that goes way beyond a typical build. But when it's you first love, you can't let her down.
Video.title: HOLLYWOOD MAKEOVER

Video.summary: The premiere episode features a dive into the influence cars had on culture of California. From road racing in the 1940’s to the inception of the iconic Woody wagon on the California beach scene, automobiles undoubtedly shaped the West Coast attitude. Ray talks to The Beach Boys frontman Mike Love and surfing icon Laird Hamilton for a glimpse into the cars that are so connected to surfing culture.
Video.title: WOODIE WAGONS

Video.summary: Ray uncovers the secrets of America’s greatest racing sport in the foothills of North Carolina. When moonshiners were looking to outrun police officers, they would custom fit engines to race the back woods tracks. That sport eventually evolved into NASCAR and Ray talks to the early adopters of this national pastime.
Video.title: MOONSHINE
